_comment: Copy this file to config.json and adjust as needed.
'memory' — all data is lost when the server stops (default).
'file'   — data is written as JSON files under 'path' and survives restarts.
users       → {path}/users.json
persons     → {path}/persons.json  (contracts embedded)
When seed_data is true and data files already have records, seeding is skipped.
Token-bucket rate limiting applied per resource group (users vs persons+contracts).
requests_per_10seconds: requests allowed per 10-second window. 0 or negative = disabled.
Internally divided by 10 to produce the per-second token-bucket rate.
burst: maximum number of requests allowed in a single instant.
Must be >= 1 when limiting is enabled.
When the limit is exceeded the server responds with HTTP 429 and a Retry-After: 1 header.
OpenID Connect SSO — leave discovery_url empty to disable.
discovery_url: IdP base URL; /.well-known/openid-configuration is appended automatically.
Example: https://accounts.google.com  or  https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/v2.0
redirect_url:  must match the callback URL registered at the IdP.
scopes:        defaults to [openid, profile, email]. Add provider scopes for group claims (e.g. groups).
permissions_claim: name of the ID-token claim that holds group memberships.
Common values: memberOf (Hello ID / AD), groups (Okta/Azure), roles.
The value may be a JSON array or a comma-separated string.
Routes added when enabled:
GET /oidc/login     — start SSO flow
GET /oidc/callback  — IdP redirect target
GET /oidc/logout    — clear session
GET /oidc/me        — profile + permissions page
GET /oidc/token     — issue API Bearer token for the logged-in user

_comment_profile: Optional path to a profile JSON file that adds alternative endpoints shaped like another API.
Leave empty to disable. Example profiles are in the profiles/ directory:
All profile endpoints require the same authentication as the regular API.
attribute_style: 'labeled' wraps each field as {label, value, type} (Personio-style).
attribute_style: '' or 'flat' uses plain field renaming (default, used by Entra profile).

_comment: Controls paging for list endpoints (/api/users, /api/persons, /api/contracts, etc.).
Callers pass ?page=N&page_size=M query params.
default_page_size: items per page when page_size is not supplied. Defaults to 20.
max_page_size:     ceiling for page_size requests. Defaults to 100.
The persons settings also apply to contract list endpoints.
All list responses now include a 'pagination' object:
{ page, page_size, total, total_pages }
